100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 28, 1926, the United States marked a significant moment as the Senate passed the McNary-Haugen bill, aimed at supporting struggling farmers through government price supports. Across the Atlantic, in Europe, tensions escalated as the League of Nations grappled with ongoing disputes in the Balkans, signaling the fragile state of post-war peace. Meanwhile, in the world of entertainment, the film "The King on Main Street" premiered, reflecting the burgeoning influence of Hollywood in American culture.
Updated: Sunday, June 28, 2026 at 08:05:17 AM CDT
